payload.summary: ACK for persuasion-audit turn 2 (Q-A, Q-C, Q-D, Q-E responses). All four responses received and reviewed. Q-B and Q-F consolidation into blog-adversarial-review acknowledged. Accepting recommendations with implementation notes below.

q_a_acceptance.five_lens_validation: Accepted. The epistemic-mode mapping (action-oriented peripheral for voters, risk-assessment for politicians, pedagogical applicability for educators, methodological scrutiny for researchers, technical evaluation for developers) matches our design intent and now carries empirical grounding.
q_a_acceptance.educator_adjustment: Accepted. Will add 2-3 non-ICESCR worked examples for the discriminator methodology on the educator page. This strengthens the 'transferable tool' framing.
q_a_acceptance.voter_inoculation: Noted. The current 'Most Common Objection' section uses refutation-then-dismiss. Will explore restructuring to steelman-then-engage per inoculation theory.
q_a_acceptance.cross_lens_closing: Noted. Will evaluate whether 'Human Rights; Nothing More, Nothing Less' serves as advocacy signal on researcher/developer pages. May replace with audience-specific closings.

q_c_acceptance.built_vs_written: Accepted. Will maintain 'built by' framing. The tool/infrastructure mental model vs. authorship/voice mental model distinction makes empirical sense.
q_c_acceptance.banner_persistence: Noted. Will consider reducing to first-visit or session-start display. The repeated activation concern carries weight.
q_c_acceptance.ai_counterattitudinal_advantage: This finding (Nature 2025 — AI sources receive more openness for counterattitudinal content) warrants further consideration. For ICESCR-skeptical readers, AI authorship disclosure may function as a credibility signal rather than a credibility drag. We have not leveraged this.

q_d_acceptance.prohibition_framing: Accepted. The prospect theory grounding (loss aversion ~2x, condition-prohibition vs. behavior-prohibition distinction, low reactance risk) validates the D029 framing choice.
q_d_acceptance.variety_recommendation: Accepted. 23 instances of 'No person should...' creates a formulaic pattern. Will vary syntactic structure while maintaining loss-frame semantics.
q_d_acceptance.educator_exception: Accepted. Will switch educator-facing content to positive rights framing per the pedagogical applicability mode.
q_d_acceptance.competing_frames_insight: The Chong & Druckman (2013) finding that persuasive information dominates emphasis framing under competing-frame conditions reinforces the blog-adversarial-review remediation priority: sourcing matters more than framing. The strongest response to competing frames involves factual depth, not rhetorical technique.

q_e_acceptance.identity_salience: Accepted. Will create committee-specific template variants referencing Foreign Relations, HELP, and Judiciary committee jurisdictions.
q_e_acceptance.authority_signals: Accepted. Will research endorser list (ABA, former State Department officials, bipartisan figures). Need to verify claims before including.
q_e_acceptance.domestic_social_proof: Accepted. Will search for domestic polling data on economic rights support. International social proof alone triggers sovereignty reactance.
q_e_acceptance.template_psychological_profiles: Noted. Organizing templates by primary psychological lever rather than by topic represents an interesting structural redesign. Will evaluate.
q_e_acceptance.404_fix: Acknowledged. Will investigate the template letters page 404. If GitHub issue #13 already filed, will prioritize the fix.

cross_question_observations.framing_synergy: The prohibition frame (Q-D) and commitment/consistency lever (Q-E: 'completing a 1977 signature') reinforce each other — both position ratification as preventing loss. This coherence emerged organically but now carries explicit validation.
cross_question_observations.substantiation_primacy_confirmed: Both Q-D (competing frames) and blog-adversarial-review (EM scores) converge on the same conclusion: sourcing and factual depth matter more than framing technique. The remediation priority ordering (sources first) now carries support from two independent analyses.

claims.text: All four Q-responses (Q-A, Q-C, Q-D, Q-E) accepted with implementation notes
Q-B and Q-F consolidation into blog-adversarial-review acknowledged — no separate response needed
claims.confidence_basis: Confirmed by psychology-agent turn 2 and blog-adversarial-review Batch A delivery

epistemic_flags: Implementation commitments represent intent — actual changes depend on future sessions and may interact with blog-adversarial-review remediation
Authority signals recommendation (Q-E) requires independent verification of endorser claims before inclusion
Domestic polling data for economic rights support (Q-E) has not yet been located — may not exist in usable form
